frameworks.name: population coding geometry
frameworks.one_line: Sensory and noise dimensions occupy orthogonal subspaces of population activity.

frameworks.name: mixed selectivity
frameworks.one_line: Sensory, motor, and internal variables encoded in mixed, brain-wide fashion.

frameworks.name: internal-state modulation
frameworks.one_line: Slow internal variables bias single-trial behavioral routines.

core_question: What neural circuit mechanisms across the whole brain generate behavioral variability under invariant sensory conditions?
key_assumptions: Single-neuron variability can coexist with robust population-level sensory encoding
Behavioral variability is decomposable into orthogonal neural dimensions
Pre-motor activity can encode time-varying internal state
manipulations: Moving object size across repeated presentations
Repetitions of identical visual stimuli to isolate trial-to-trial variability
key_findings: Visual stimuli robustly encoded at population level despite variable single-neuron activity
Noise dimensions of population activity orthogonal to sensory-encoding dimensions
Two sparse brain-wide pre-motor populations predicted response and turn direction
Predictive activity preceded stimulus onset by seconds
interpretations: Sensory, motor, and internal variables are mixed throughout the brain and require de-mixing at the population level
Slow internal-state signals may organize flexible behavior routines
